core_payload.content:
# User Preferences

## 1. Role
User Preferences is the richer behavioral contract for the second brain, allowing planning, notifications, autonomy, and assistant behavior to adapt to the person with far more nuance than baseline settings allow.

## 2. Preference Contract
This layer governs when the platform should speak, stay quiet, wake the human, or let the swarm continue safely. It shapes sleep posture, quiet windows, assistant style, autonomy tolerance, review thresholds, and intervention behavior as explicit operating law.

## 3. Operational Consequence
Human rhythm remains central even as the system becomes more capable. Planning, night autonomy, reminder pressure, and control-surface behavior align to one durable person-specific contract instead of generic automation defaults.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
User Preferences supports To Dig Deep by teaching deep systems how to serve human rhythm rather than forcing humans to adapt to machine convenience.

neuro_concentrate.summary: User Preferences defines the behavioral contract that lets N1Hub adapt planning, notifications, assistant style, and autonomy posture to one actual person instead of generic defaults. It covers quiet windows, sleep posture, intervention timing, review thresholds, and tolerance for autonomous continuation across the system. The capsule matters because deeper automation remains trustworthy only when it respects human rhythm, attention, and interruption cost. It turns personal operating boundaries into explicit platform law rather than scattered settings or operator folklore.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Stripped future-meta phrasing so the capsule states present preference doctrine directly.
